G10 REGIME COMPOSITE

REGIME READ ▶
EUR/USD remains locked in neutral territory with minimal conviction (5% confidence), despite the US-Germany 10Y spread maintaining a +1.27pp premium in favor of dollars. The driver is structurally sound—the spread reflects genuine monetary policy divergence—but positioning sits at only the 35.3rd percentile of historical ranges, meaning speculative longs remain markedly underweight. Volatility has risen to the 75.7th percentile, indicating price discovery is active even as directional commitment is absent. This combination suggests traders are hedging rather than trending; the pair oscillates around levels where neither side commands conviction.

G10 REGIME COMPOSITE
Composite Score-16NEUTRAL
REGIME READ ▶
USD/JPY displays textbook neutral positioning with 16% confidence and the lowest volatility footprint across majors (23.1st percentile). The US-Japan 10Y spread of +1.92pp is the widest among G10 pairs, yet positioning sits near the historical median (42.9th percentile), implying the market has fully priced the policy divergence. This combination of strong structural support (the spread) and balanced tactical positioning (percentile near 50) creates a stable equilibrium. The pair is range-bound by design: bulls lack fresh catalysts to extend, while the BoJ's forward guidance keeps shorts cautious about acceleration.

REGIME READ ▶
USD/INR has shifted into MODERATE DEPRECIATION PRESSURE (31% confidence), marking the only directional regime call today. The US-India 10Y spread has collapsed to -2.90pp, meaning Indian yields now offer a 290bp carry advantage—but market mechanics are skewed by persistent RBI intervention. Volatility trades at the 95.9th percentile, the highest across all three pairs, reflecting the tension between fundamental depreciation pressure and official sector buying. This is a classic emerging market regime: carry attraction in yields (negative spread) competes against currency weakness, and policy authorities actively defend levels. Precision entries are inadvisable; this is a directional allocation decision, not a tactical trade.
